steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
The sentence pairs two countries with two prices: 62 USD in one location and 110 USD in Luxembourg (marked by “respectively”).

The blank comes right after “OECD” and must allow the sentence to name which OECD nations are being priced.

The phrase “and Luxembourg” follows immediately, so the blank must complete “OECD ______” as a single unit that identifies the first nation or nations.

steps.title: Apply the appositive punctuation rule
steps.content:
“Chile and Luxembourg” functions as a restrictive appositive identifying which OECD nations are meant, so no punctuation is needed between “nations” and “Chile.” Choice A inserts a comma that would treat the names as nonessential, choice B adds an unwarranted semicolon, and choice C uses a colon that interrupts the noun phrase.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
